purpose: Binary Yes/No decisions for each canonical ESRS topic, using final SpecificTopic Yes tags and source evidence to choose the closest ESRS topics.

rules: Answer every ESRS label as Yes or No.
Emit only ESRS labels answered Yes in final_closest_esrs_topics.
ESRS is downstream of final SpecificTopic decisions; do not independently invent ESRS labels from raw keyword hits.
ESRS 1 and ESRS 2 require actual sustainability-reporting/disclosure architecture, not generic ESG relevance.

ESRS G1 requires business conduct, corruption/bribery, whistleblowing, lobbying, supplier payment practices, corporate culture, or central compliance architecture; ordinary legal compliance is not enough.
Choose closest ESRS topics. Do not map every plausible environmental/social domain when the source evidence is narrower.

tag_decisions.nearby_label_if_any: optional ESRS label that better fits the evidence

final_closest_esrs_topics: all ESRS topics from tag_decisions with answer=Yes, after closest-topic pruning

topic_to_esrs_crosswalk.instruction: Use as a controlled projection from final SpecificTopic values. Then verify against source text and ESRS definitions.

Agricultural Pollution: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Child Labor: ESRS S2: Workers in the Value Chain
Circular Economy: ESRS E5: Resource Use and Circular Economy
Coastal Zone Management: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Drought Management: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Electrical Equipment: ESRS E5: Resource Use and Circular Economy
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR): ESRS E5: Resource Use and Circular Economy
Fisheries Management: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Forced Labor and Human Trafficking: ESRS S2: Workers in the Value Chain
Marine Environment Protection: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Marine Pollution: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Marine Protected Areas: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Stormwater Management: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Transboundary Water Management: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Waste Reduction and Recycling: ESRS E5: Resource Use and Circular Economy
Wastewater Treatment: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Water Allocation: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Water Conservation: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Water Infrastructure: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Water Pricing: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Water Quality Standards: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Water Resource Management: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources
Water Use Efficiency: ESRS E3: Water and Marine Resources

tags.definition: Cross-cutting requirements for sustainability statements, including reporting principles, double materiality, value-chain coverage, time horizons, presentation, and preparation basis.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS 1: General Requirements"?
yes_when: The source centrally establishes general sustainability-reporting architecture, reporting principles, materiality assessment, value-chain coverage, or basis of preparation rather than a topical ESG matter.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.

Evidence-packet support for this tag is weak, indirect, or absent; answer Yes only when the current source text independently and directly satisfies the definition, decision question, and a yes_when rule.
no_when: The source is merely a topical environmental/social/governance law or a disclosure rule that maps more directly to ESRS 2 or a topical ESRS.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.
Do not answer Yes from analogy to weak master examples, broad ESG relatedness, a title-only hunch, or the absence of a better label.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "The source centrally establishes general sustainability-reporting architecture, reporting principles, materiality assessment, value-chain coverage, or basis of preparation rather than a topical ESG matter.". Should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS 1: General Requirements"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source centrally establishes general sustainability-reporting architecture, reporting principles, materiality assessment, value-chain coverage, or basis of preparation rather than a topical ESG matter.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "general requirements" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The source centrally establishes general sustainability-reporting architecture, reporting principles, materiality assessment, value-chain coverage, or basis of preparation rather than a topical ESG m…". Should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS 1: General Requirements"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source is merely a topical environmental/social/governance law or a disclosure rule that maps more directly to ESRS 2 or a topical ESRS.". Should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS 1: General Requirements"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

tags.definition: Cross-cutting disclosure requirements on governance, strategy, impact/risk/opportunity management, metrics, targets, policies, actions, and entity-wide sustainability information.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS 2: General Disclosures"?
yes_when: The source centrally requires general sustainability or non-financial disclosures, governance/strategy disclosures, IRO-process disclosures, policies/actions/targets disclosure, or reporting architecture not limited to one topic.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The source is operational regulation with reporting to regulators only, or a topic-specific law without public sustainability/general disclosure architecture.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "the protection of the consumer requires that all producers involved in the production process should be made liable". Should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS S4: Consumers and End-users"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The final topics/source mechanisms regulate product/service users, consumer rights, product labels, safety, recalls, privacy/data protection, accessibility, or end-user inclusion.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "consumers" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The final topics/source mechanisms regulate product/service users, consumer rights, product labels, safety, recalls, privacy/data protection, accessibility, or end-user inclusion.". Should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS S4: Consumers and End-users"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source regulates product manufacturers, chemicals, or reporting without consumer/end-user impact or information/safety/privacy duties.". Should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS S4: Consumers and End-users"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

test_case.question: The master evidence includes a weak/indirect edge example: "in the access to and supply of goods and services". Standing alone, should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS S4: Consumers and End-users"?
test_case.reasoning_check: The master reason was classified as weak/cautionary; the LLM must look for central legal duties, not rely on weak contextual association.

tags.definition: Corporate culture, business ethics, anti-corruption, anti-bribery, whistleblower protection, political engagement/lobbying, supplier relationships, payment practices, and conduct incidents.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS G1: Business Conduct"?
yes_when: The final topics/source mechanisms concern anti-corruption/bribery, business conduct, corporate culture, whistleblowing, lobbying, supplier payment practices, or compliance/corporate-governance conduct.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The source is only environmental/social/product regulation with ordinary compliance obligations and no business-conduct mechanism.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "Article 5 Penalties 1. test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The final topics/source mechanisms concern anti-corruption/bribery, business conduct, corporate culture, whistleblowing, lobbying, supplier payment practices, or compliance/corporate-governance conduct.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

Should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS G1: Business Conduct"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source is only environmental/social/product regulation with ordinary compliance obligations and no business-conduct mechanism.". Should the LLM tag ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS G1: Business Conduct"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

test_case.question: The master evidence includes a weak/indirect edge example: "All economic operators are expected to act responsibly and in full accordance with the legal requirements applicable when placing or making products available on the market.". tags.tag: ESRS S2: Workers in the Value Chain
tags.definition: Workers outside the entity's own workforce in upstream or downstream value chains, including supplier, contractor, platform, migrant, forced-labor, child-labor, and trafficking-related worker impacts.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS S2: Workers in the Value Chain"?
yes_when: The final topics/source mechanisms explicitly concern value-chain/supply-chain workers, supplier labor due diligence, forced labor, child labor, trafficking, or labor conditions outside own workforce.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The law applies to employers and their own employees generally; use S1 unless value-chain worker scope is explicit.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

tags.definition: Internal/custom ESRS-like label for sustainability matters where technological innovation itself is the central regulated or disclosed topic.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged ClosestESRSTopics = "ESRS T1: Technological Innovation"?
yes_when: The source centrally regulates or requires disclosure of technological innovation, clean-tech innovation, R&D innovation, digital innovation, or innovation incentives as the main sustainability mechanism.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: Technology is merely a tool used to comply with climate, pollution, privacy, energy, or reporting rules; map to the topical ESRS instead.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.
